business.headline: Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

Waiting for Form 16? Here's when you'll receive it and why it matters for your ITRThe countdown to ITR filing has begun, and Form 16 remains one of the most important documents for salaried taxpayers. Knowing when your employer must issue it and what information it contains can help you prepare for a smoother tax filing experience.

Sensex ends 382 points higher, Nifty nears 23,500 led by IT sector rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 382.50 points, or 0.52%, to close at 74,649.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 100.95 points, or 0.43%, to settle at 23,483.55.

From 5th to 7th: Why India is losing the stock market race against Asian rivalsIndia has slipped from fifth to seventh in global stock market rankings as South Korea and Taiwan moved ahead. The shift shows investors are favouring AI-linked semiconductor hubs over markets with limited listed chipmakers.

8th Pay Commission deadline extended again: What it means for your salary and arrearsAnother extension, more questions. The 8th Pay Commission has pushed back its submission deadline again, keeping employees guessing about pay hikes and arrears.

Will your home loan EMI rise again? What RBI's June MPC meet could mean for borrowersIf you have a home loan, the RBI's June policy meeting could be worth watching closely. Borrowers are eager to know whether their EMIs will remain stable or face another change in the months ahead.

Why is Nvidia hiring when the rest of Silicon Valley is firingNvidia is expanding H-1B hiring even as major technology rivals cut jobs and slow foreign recruitment. The move stands out for Indian professionals facing layoffs, visa pressure and a tighter US market.

Why your society maintenance bill could soon be Rs 3,000 higher every monthFor many apartment residents, the next surprise could arrive not in their power bill or fuel bill, but in their society maintenance statement. Rising wage costs and higher operating expenses are forcing several housing societies to consider significant fee hikes.

India-US trade talks begin today: Why Section 301 could become the biggest issueIndian and US negotiators have begun fresh trade talks in New Delhi with Section 301 emerging as a central issue. India wants assurance that future US tariff action will not erode gains from a bilateral trade deal.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech jump up to 6%. 3 reasons why IT stocks are rallying today?IT stocks extended gains for a third straight session, led by Infosys, TCS, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra. The rally reflects stronger global tech sentiment, AI spending optimism, rate-cut hopes and support from a weaker rupee.

After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India in global stock market rankingsSouth Korea has moved past India in global stock market rankings after a sharp rally in AI-linked chipmakers. The shift underlines how investors are favouring markets tied to the AI supply chain while Indian equities face near-term pressures.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,300; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 302.16 points, or 0.41%, to 73,965.18 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 107.05 points, or 0.46%, to 23,275.55 as of 9:25 am.

Trump cuts tariffs on farm, industrial equipment from 25% to 15%. What it meansUS President Donald Trump has lowered US tariffs on selected farm and industrial equipment from 25% to 15% through December 2027. The move couples cheaper imports with incentives for greater use of American steel and aluminium.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

India may reconsider Scotch whisky tariff cuts if UK imposes steel curbsIndia could revisit tariff concessions granted to Britain on products such as Scotch whisky if London fails to address New Delhi's concerns over proposed steel restrictions, according to an Indian official.

Can small-cap funds really deliver higher long-term returns?While small caps have delivered better returns than large caps over the past two decades, the difference may be smaller than many investors expect, says a recent study.

SIP investing, the RCB way18 years. That's how long RCB supporters waited for glory. If patience eventually paid off in cricket, could the same principle be the secret behind successful SIP investing?

What is Section 301 of the US Trade Act? Why India wants protection from itAs negotiations enter the final stage, one issue attracting attention is India's effort to secure protection from possible US tariffs under Section 301 of the US Trade Act, 1974.

Piyush Goyal says 99% of India-US trade deal talks completedSpeaking about the ongoing negotiations, Goyal said discussions between the two sides would continue from June 2 to June 4 to resolve a few remaining issues before the agreement is formally announced.

entertainment.headline: Success brings enemies: Kangana Ranaut backs Ranveer Singh amid Don 3 ban row Kangana Ranaut backed Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 ban row. She said success often brings enemies and obstacles.

Euphoria 3 finale: Not just Rue, the series failed all its women As Euphoria reached its final stretch, its women increasingly became symbols rather than people. That shift left the series facing questions about whether it examined female experience or merely packaged it as spectacle.

Ram Charan's lookalike fan causes chaos at Peddi event, gets surprise reward later A fan dressed like Ram Charan rushed towards the stage at the Peddi pre-release event in Vijayawada before being stopped by the actor's personal security guard. The lookalike fan was later allowed to meet the actor.

Diljit Dosanjh's Aroma song leaves bitter aftertaste with use of transgender slur Diljit Dosanjh is facing criticism over a lyric in Aroma that listeners say uses a slur against transgender people. The row intensified at the start of Pride Month, with users questioning the message and the influence of major artists.

Comedian Sumukhi Suresh on bid to stay relevant: Pulling my hair every day Comedian Sumukhi Suresh opened up about representing India at the Netflix Is A Joke Festival in Los Angeles and the emotional moment she learned about the opportunity. She also spoke about Hormonal, Pushpavalli, The Royals 2 and staying relevant.

Rashmika Mandanna, Kriti Sanon and question of 'pehla pyaar' at Cocktail 2 launch Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon, the new Cocktail girls in Bollywood, discussed the idea of first love at the trailer launch of their film. And there's more to the story.

Toxic star Yash video-calls fan battling cancer, offers hope and support Kannada star Yash took a moment from his busy schedule to video-call a fan battling cancer, offering hope and support. His warm interaction and assurance have deeply touched fans across social media.

Rs 84 in account, no auto fare: How Dhurandhar's Gaurav Gera survived struggle days Gaurav Gera has recalled the years before Dhurandhar, when he survived on Rs 84 in his bank account in Mumbai. He said family support, self-respect and caution against arrogance shaped his journey.

Deepika holds back tears, Ranveer enjoys traditions in new pics from family wedding Deepika Padukone got emotional, while Ranveer Singh participated in rituals at his cousin's wedding. The viral photos from the ceremony have renewed attention to the couple's personal life amid news about their professional projects.

Triptii Dimri, Madhuri Dixit on women being judged in cinema: It's not fair Triptii Dimri and Madhuri Dixit spoke about the scrutiny they have faced as Maa Behen nears release. Their accounts from different generations underline how women in cinema are still judged more harshly.

Cocktail 2 trailer: Friendship, flirt, fabulous looks in Shahid-Kriti-Rashmika film The trailer of Cocktail 2 introduces a romantic drama starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, set against a picturesque Italy backdrop. The film explores a complicated love triangle filled with friendship, jealousy and emotional conflict.

Caretaker held for stealing Rs 25 lakh from Raveena Tandon's brother's house Actor Raveena Tandon's family has been caught in a theft case after Juhu police arrested a woman for allegedly stealing jewellery and valuables from the residence of her brother, Rajiv Tandon.

Trisha shares witty post as link-up rumours with CM Vijay continue to fuel trolls Actor Trisha Krishnan shared an Instagram Story featuring her dog with a pointed caption. The post drew notice as online speculation around her equation with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay continued.

Kamal Haasan production house video fuels Rajinikanth-Ashwath buzz. Watch Raaj Kamal Films International's birthday video for director Ashwath Marimuthu has reignited talk that he may direct Rajinikanth's Thalaivar 173. The buzz follows earlier changes in the film's direction team, though no official confirmation has been issued.

What really ended Sushmita Sen-Lalit Modi relationship? He reveals breakup reason Businessman Lalit Modi has opened up on the reason behind his break-up with actor Sushmita Sen. He also defended her against the gold digger jibes, calling her self-made and generous.

Mika Abdalla, Jake Short end engagement amid Off Campus buzz Mika Abdalla and Jake Short have ended their engagement. The Off Campus actor's representative confirmed that the duo remain on friendly terms and have asked for privacy.

Kala Hiran is not Salman Khan's biopic: Producer Amit Jani on legal notice Amit Jani has called Salman Khan's legal notice against Kala Hiran premature and said the film is not a biopic on the actor. He said the film draws from public-domain events and foregrounds the Bishnoi community's link with wildlife conservation.

Pranit More's viral 'peak Gurgaon content' is actually peak rape-culture thinking The problem with the latest 'joke' on Pranit More's stand-up show is not just the fact that it was said, but how it was received: with applauses, laughter, amplification and glorification. Because buying a Rs 370 biryani is not the same as getting a woman's consent.

Chunnari Chunnari row grows as composers deny remake tag, call it homage The composers of Chunnari Chunnari - Let's Go say the song is a homage to the Biwi No 1 original, not a remake. The clarification comes amid social media backlash and a Rs 400 crore legal fight over song rights.

general news.headline: CBSE Chairman, Secretary shunted out amid national uproar over OSM fiascoThe Centre has initiated major action amid the CBSE OSM controversy, transferring the Board's Chairman and Secretary and constituting an inquiry committee to examine the procurement of On-Screen Marking services. The move comes after weeks of questions over evaluation processes, portal issues and vendor selection.

Will fight or die: Mamata holds massive dharna, only 5 MLAs, 3 MPs attendMamata Banerjee staged a dharna in central Kolkata and accused the BJP of attacking Trinamool leaders and intimidating the party. The protest marked her return to street politics after the Bengal election loss, even as only five party MLAs attended amid buzz of a split.

Final push that made Annamalai plan BJP exit, form new partyDynamic IPS officer-turned-politician K Annamalai is planning to quit the BJP and launch a party. While his exit might appear to be sudden, it comes amid a series of developments ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. Here's what might have been the final push.

Resign, Balen: Nepal students, Opposition protest against PM's India remarkPrime Minister Balen Shah's remark that Nepal too might have crossed into Indian territory has triggered a political firestorm, seemingly uniting a fractured opposition, now asking for his resignation. Balen, who rode a wave of protests to become the PM, is himself facing huge protests.

CBSE website security is zero, says IT veteran amid marking portal controversyBy now it is a fact that the CBSE website, or at least the one that existed around a week ago, is poorly secured. But just how poorly? India Today Tech spoke to an IT industry veteran and digital rights activist. On a scale of 1 to 10, he rated the security of CBSE website zero!

Live: BJP rigged 177 of 294 seats in Bengal polls, says Mamata at protest siteBengal TMC crisis live updates: In her first public address to the people of Bengal following the TMC's poll defeat, Mamata Banerjee launched a scathing attack against both the ruling BJP and the suspended party MLAs who had recently filed a complaint with the assembly speaker over a signature forgery issue. During the address, Banerjee accused the ruling BJP of executing a targeted political vendetta against TMC workers. She further alleged that police personnel are operating entirely at the behest of the BJP government, actively threatening workers and pressuring them to defect from the party. Concluding her remarks, Banerjee asserted that TMC workers are being subjected to widespread post-poll violence orchestrated by the BJP with the direct assistance of the state administration. To catch every detail from the protest march, catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in

Siddaramaiah to retain CM bungalow, DK Shivakumar will move to another govt houseSiddaramaiah will continue in the Cauvery bungalow even as DK Shivakumar takes over as Chief Minister. The arrangement leaves the official residence unchanged while the new Chief Minister shifts to another government house.

After delay, monsoon to reach Kerala on June 4; very heavy rainfall expectedThe weather agency has forecast widespread rainfall activity across the southern states over the next week, with Kerala expected to bear the brunt of the monsoon's arrival.

Will 50 MLAs split TMC, claim symbol? Secret hotel meeting sparks buzzThe threat of a split within the Trinamool Congress is for real and Mamata Banerjee could lose the party's control and symbol. Suspended TMC spokesperson Riju Dutta said that around 50 MLAs, enough to tide over the anti-defection law, had held a secret meeting, and were backing Ritabrata Banerjee as their leader.

I cracked 5 hackathons and earned Rs 8 lakh before completing my second year of BTechAgnik Misra is a second-year BTech student in Computer Science and Engineering. In just 24 months, he has won five hackathons, secured multiple internships, and earned nearly Rs 8 lakh. He is also working on his own startup.

Cigarette burn marks, 51 injuries found on Kerala toddler; stepfather in custodyWhat began as a claim that a toddler choked while being fed has unravelled into a disturbing murder investigation, with a postmortem exposing dozens of injuries and triggering public outrage.

No role for 3rd parties in border row: India after Nepal PM's 'encroached' remarkMEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said boundary-related matters between India and Nepal are strictly bilateral and are being addressed through existing mechanisms established by the two countries.

Why BJP rolled out red carpet for chief of Balen Shah's partyNepal's ruling party chief Rabi Lamichhane met BJP leaders after a rare ceremonial welcome at the party headquarters. The outreach, which comes amid border tensions under new PM Balen Shah, suggests both sides want to keep communication channels open.

As Pak turns US stooge, Afghan Taliban lean on Russia; history comes full circleWith Pakistan drawing closer to the US and cross-border tensions simmering, the Taliban is seemingly seeking new allies. Its latest agreement with Russia, focussed on restoring Soviet- and Russian-made military hardware, signals a deeper partnership between Kabul and Moscow.

CBSE's Rs 384-crore OSM deal: How Coempt beat TCS by 2 points and a 60% lower bidTender documents show Coempt Edu Teck beat TCS for CBSE's digital evaluation contract. The details have come under focus as CBSE's OSM platform faces criticism over delays and technical issues.

How a signature forgery row has pushed Trinamool to breaking pointA forged signature row over the Leader of the Opposition post has triggered expulsions and secret meetings in the Trinamool Congress. The controversy, which comes on the back of deepening dissent within the party, has intensified the buzz of a vertical split.

Virat, Anushka seek Premanand Maharaj's blessings after RCB's IPL win. PicsAnushka Sharma and Virat Kohli visited Vrindavan to seek blessings from Premanand Maharaj. The visit comes after RCB's IPL title win for the second time.

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

1 lakh file access attempts: CBSE says barrage of cyberattacks on re-evaluation portalMore than 16,000 students applied for CBSE re-evaluation within hours of the portal's launch, even as it faced alleged cyberattacks, including 1.5 million hits in two minutes and over one lakh unauthorised access attempts.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find himIPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Cocktail 2 trailer: Friendship, flirt, fabulous looks in Shahid-Kriti-Rashmika filmThe trailer of Cocktail 2 introduces a romantic drama starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, set against a picturesque Italy backdrop. The film explores a complicated love triangle filled with friendship, jealousy and emotional conflict.

Do judges need so many breaks? A look at an SC judge's day to settle the debateThe Supreme Court has entered a six-week summer recess with only the vacation benches hearing urgent cases. The break has kickstarted the annual ritualistic debate over judicial vacations amid high pendency. Here's a look at an SC judge's day to settle the debate.

I grew up romanticising DDLJ's Raj. Off Campus just raised the barAs a 90s rom-com fan, Prime Video's Off Campus changed how I see screen romance. The male characters on the show listen, respect consent and honour boundaries, challenging the toxic behaviours many of us grew up accepting as normal in reel and real life.

Reputed IIT or dream branch? The dilemma facing 56,880 JEE Advanced qualifiersOver 56,000 students have qualified JEE Advanced 2026, but the toughest decision may still lie ahead. As JoSAA counselling begins, students must weigh IIT reputation, branch preference, placements, campus life and future career goals before locking their choices.

sports.headline: Gautam Gambhir's coaching unit gets new hand for young spinners The BCCI has appointed Sairaj Bahutule as India's spin-bowling coach under Gautam Gambhir. He will mentor a young group of spinners as India try to rebuild their home Test edge.

Snoop Dogg's gospel: Why 19-year-old Andreeva always thanks herself at Slams Mirra Andreeva swept past Sorana Cirstea to reach another French Open semi-final and then explained her habit of thanking herself after big wins. The victory added a teenage Roland Garros record to her run.

PV Sindhu returns to top 10 in badminton rankings after nearly 3 years PV Sindhu returned to the world top 10 and beat Busanan Ongbamrungphan in straight games at the Indonesia Open. The result reinforced her recent revival before a fresh test against world No. 1 An Se-young.

India favourites for T20 World Cup: Ex-Indian captain makes bold claim Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Anjum Chopra has backed India to begin the T20 World Cup in England as one of the favourites. Her view rests on the squad's growing familiarity with conditions and the need for a more flexible batting order.

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World Cup FIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

Quarterfinals live: Zeverev fights back to break Jodar in opening set French Open 2026 live score: 7th seed Elena Svitolina has crashed out of the Roland Garros after losing to 23-year-old Marta Kostyuk in the quarter-final of the tournament. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev is taking on Rafael Jodar in the men's singles quarterfinals.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland face tricky continental group FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland head a volatile Group B featuring Bosnia and Herzegovina, Canada and Qatar. The section will turn on veteran leaders, injury concerns and very little margin for error.

Man City's Rodri to decide future after World Cup as Real Madrid interest grows FIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri has parked questions over his Manchester City future until after Spain's World Cup campaign. His message to Spain is clear: work humbly, chase every ball, and ignore the noise.

After selection uproar, Auqib Nabi joins India's Test squad but with a twist IND vs AFG One-off Test: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Test squad sparked widespread debate, but the BCCI has now handed the Jammu and Kashmir pacer an unexpected opportunity, offering a twist to the selection story.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find him IPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Roland Garros Day 10 Order of Play: Zverev and next-gen stars take centre stageRoland Garros moves into a loaded day with Alexander Zverev, Elena Svitolina and Joao Fonseca all on the schedule. The draw also carries a Ukrainian derby, a late-night clash of disruptors and Indian interest in boys doubles.

Virat Kohli, RCB fired up as Mo Bobat calls for unprecedented IPL featIPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru ignited dreams of an unprecedented IPL three-peat after team director Mo Bobat delivered a stirring dressing-room rallying cry following their 2026 title victory over Gujarat Titans.

PV Sindhu wins Indonesia Open opener, potential An Se-young clash aheadPV Sindhu opened her Indonesia Open campaign with a straight-games win over Busanan Ongbamrungphan. The result sends her into the next round, where world No. 1 An Se-young is the likely challenge.

Franchise cricket's boom forces ICC to take action on jam-packed calendarThe ICC has set up a committee to examine how the surge of franchise leagues can coexist with the international calendar. The move reflects growing concern that bilateral cricket is losing ground as players and boards follow the T20 money.

Arnaldi outlasts Tiafoe in 5-hour epic to seal historic Italian trio in ParisRoland Garros: Matteo Arnaldi outlasted Frances Tiafoe in a five-set French Open epic to reach the quarter-finals. The comeback completed a rare Italian trio in the last eight and set up a meeting with Matteo Berrettini.

Sabalenka and Osaka unite after Roland Garros clash, want women in prime-time slotsRoland Garros 2026: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka formed a united front at French Open, demanding more prime-time visibility for women's tennis after their historic fourth-round night clash ended a three-year shutout.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget herRajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Sabalenka aces Michael Jackson's moonwalk after beating Osaka: VideoAryna Sabalenka beat Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-3 to reach the French Open quarter-finals before breaking into a moonwalk on Court Philippe-Chatrier. The celebratory flourish capped another commanding display from the World No. 1.

Contract crisis hits ISL as futures of nearly 150 players hang in the balanceNearly 150 ISL players became free agents after their contracts expired amid the AIFF-club stand-off over next season. The ongoing uncertainty has weakened players' bargaining power and hampered clubs' preparations for the upcoming campaign.

Berrettini avenges Jannik Sinner, knocks Cerundolo out in straight setsItalian Matteo Berrettini avenged his compatriot Jannik Sinner in the French Open after beating young Juan Manuel Cerundolo on Monday, June 1. Cerundolo's impressive run in the tournament was stopped in the fourth round.

World Cup: South Africa squad departs without assistant coach after US visa delaySouth Africa's World Cup squad departed for Mexico on Monday, 24 hours later than scheduled, after visa complications disrupted the team's travel plans and left assistant coach Helman Mkhalele stranded in Johannesburg.

Thomas Tuchel backs England to overcome heat and humidity at FIFA World CupEngland manager Thomas Tuchel has backed his side to cope with the heat and humidity expected at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, insisting the challenging conditions in North America will be an obstacle to overcome rather than an excuse for underperformance.

Mischievous RCB stars pour ice-cold water on coach after IPL winRoyal Challengers Bengaluru celebrated their IPL win in style at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. RCB players Tim David and Salt emptied the entire ice bucket on head coach Andy Flower after the final.

Tim David suspended for RCB's IPL 2027 opener after ice-bag incident with umpireRoyal Challengers Bengaluru batter Tim David has been suspended for the franchise's opening match of IPL 2027 after breaching the league's Code of Conduct during the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Serena Williams confirms shock tennis comeback at 44 for Queen's ChampionshipsSerena Williams has confirmed her return to professional tennis at the age of 44, nearly four years after her last competitive appearance. Williams has accepted a doubles' wildcard at the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club in London.

Balaji-Demoliner reach doubles quarters, Arnav Paparkar enters boys' last 16N. Sriram Balaji and Brazil's Marcelo Demoliner advanced to the men's doubles quarterfinals of the French Open on Monday, while Arnav Paparkar continued India's strong showing by reaching the boys' singles round of 16 in the junior competition.

Don't need to prove it to anybody: Satwik-Chirag just want Badminton to flourishSingapore Open 2026: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty ended their two-year title wait by winning the Singapore Open. Their response afterwards was pointed: the win settled nothing for them, but renewed a call for sport to be recognised and supported properly.

French Open fines player Rs 71 lakh for sexist remark on refereeParaguayan tennis player Adolfo Vallejo has been fined 65,000 euros by the French Open after making sexist remarks about chair umpire Ana Carvalho. The controversy stems from his dramatic second-round defeat at Roland Garros.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A preview: Can Mexico finally end their quarterfinal jinx?FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A Preview: The stage is finally set for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with Group A kicking off things. Mexico start as the hot favourites but South Korea, Czechia and South Africa will be aiming to disrupt the party for the co-hosts.

ICC approves trial use of pink ball in Tests hit by bad lightThe usage of pink balls on a trial basis during Test matches in case bad light affects play, and allowing head coaches inside the field of play during drink breaks, are among the major changes to the playing conditions approved by the International Cricket Council (ICC) Board at its annual meeting in Ahmedabad.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
